question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicMapper.map() uses MapStruct's automatic field mapping without null-safety guards on entity.autoRenewAccountId, causing a NullPointerException that reveals internal class structure useful for crafting further Hashgraph history manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @GetMapping on getTopic() produces APPLICATION_JSON without any ETag or Last-Modified header, preventing clients from detecting when the mirror node's cached Hashgraph history has been silently updated or tampered with between requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityIdNumParameter.valueOfNullable() uses CommonProperties.getInstance() as a singleton to read shard/realm defaults, and if those defaults are misconfigured or stale, cause all topic ID resolutions to silently map to the wrong shard's Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a topic ID with a realm value of 0 and a very large num to getTopic(), causing EntityId.of(shard, 0, largeNum) to produce a getId() that collides with a topic in realm 1 with a smaller num, returning that topic's Hashgraph history due to the ID encoding scheme?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicMapper.map() maps entity.id to topicId without verifying that entity.id equals topic.id, so that if the entity table and topic table have diverged (e.g., due to a partial mirror node sync), the response presents a topicId that does not match the actual Hashgraph-recorded topic identity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to perform a high-volume enumeration attack, systematically harvesting the Hashgraph-recorded createdTimestamp and timestampRange of all topics to reconstruct the full historical timeline of the ledger?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopic() does not validate that the Topic domain object returned by topicService.findById() has the same entity ID as the Entity returned by entityService.findById(), allowing a database inconsistency to produce a response where the topic's Hashgraph history fields (e.g., memo, submitKey) belong to a different topic than the topicId field indicates?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ENTITY_ID_REGEX allows a realm value up to 5 digits (99999) while Hedera's canonical realm is 0, causing getTopic() to accept IDs from non-existent realms and potentially resolve to unintended database rows that contain stale or manipulated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicMapper.map() maps entity.timestampRange to the Topic response's timestamp without verifying the range's lower bound is a valid Hashgraph consensus timestamp (positive, non-zero), allowing a corrupted database entry with a zero or negative timestamp to be surfaced as authoritative Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopic() assembles the Topic response from three separate database reads (topicRepository, entityRepository, customFeeRepository) without a transaction boundary, so that a concurrent mirror node update between reads causes the response to reflect a mix of pre-update and post-update Hashgraph history states?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityIdNumParameter.valueOf() does not validate that the parsed shard matches the mirror node's configured shard, allowing cross-shard topic ID queries that return Hashgraph history records from a different network's ledger state stored in the same database?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicMapper.map() uses the @Mapping annotation to map entity.createdTimestamp with QUALIFIER_TIMESTAMP without bounds-checking, allowing a future timestamp (greater than current consensus time) stored in the database to be returned as the topic's Hashgraph-recorded creation time, implying a falsified history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @RequestMapping on TopicController produces APPLICATION_JSON without any integrity signature or Merkle proof in the response, allowing a man-in-the-middle attacker to modify the returned Hashgraph history (createdTimestamp, timestampRange, topicId) without the client being able to detect the tampering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicService.findById() calls topicRepository.findById(id.getId()) where getId() encodes shard, realm, and num into a single long, and if two different shard.realm.num combinations produce the same getId() value, cause the endpoint to return the wrong topic's Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityIdNumParameter.valueOfNullable() parses the num component from matcher.group(5) without validating it against the maximum allowed entity number on the Hashgraph network, allowing queries for non-existent future entity IDs that could match database rows inserted by a tampered mirror node ingestion process?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopic() does not verify that the topic returned by topicService.findById() is of entity type TOPIC (as opposed to ACCOUNT, TOKEN, etc.), allowing a database row with a misclassified entity type to be returned as a topic's Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicMapper.map() maps entity.autoRenewAccountId to the Topic response's autoRenewAccount field without verifying the account exists on the Hashgraph ledger, allowing a stale or tampered autoRenewAccountId to be presented as part of the topic's authoritative history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @GetMapping on getTopic() has no CORS restriction, allowing a malicious web page to make cross-origin requests to GET /api/v1/topics/{id} and harvest Hashgraph history data (createdTimestamp, timestampRange, adminKey) from authenticated users' browser sessions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityIdNumParameter.valueOf() throws IllegalArgumentException with the message 'Invalid entity ID' for malformed inputs, and by fuzzing the path variable, enumerate which ID formats are accepted versus rejected to map out the ID space of valid Hashgraph topics for targeted history harvesting?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicMapper.map() uses MapStruct's generated code to combine CustomFee, Entity, and Topic objects, and if MapStruct's generated mapper has a field-ordering bug, cause fields from one object to overwrite fields from another, producing a Topic response with a falsified Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopic() does not validate that the entity.id returned by entityService.findById() matches the id.id() used in the request, allowing a database-level ID aliasing or collision to cause the response to present a different entity's Hashgraph history under the queried topic ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ENTITY_ID_REGEX allows shard values up to 4 digits (9999) while Hedera currently uses shard 0, causing getTopic() to accept IDs with arbitrary shard values that may resolve to unintended database rows containing manipulated Hashgraph history from a different network?
